name: Five of Swords
effect: You take 5d6 necrotic damage that ignores resistances and cannot be prevented by other means.

name: Seven of Swords
effect: You take 5d10 cold damage that ignores resistances and cannot be undone by other means. If this damage reduces your hits to 0, you turn into an ice sculpture that can be thawed with open fire or spells. If you spend more than 1 minute unthawed, you die.

name: Nine of Swords
effect: You cast a fireball spell centered on yourself, equivalent to if you cast it with a 5th level slot (DC 15).

name: Five of Stars
effect: You cultivate a huge amount of roots and thorny vines around yourself. For 5 minutes, a circle with 20 feet radius around you is considered difficult terrain.

name: Seven of Stars
effect: You summon a Polar bear that is charmed by you and agrees to obey your verbal commands for 1 hour. The bear becomes hostile afterwards.

name: Nine of Stars
effect: You summon a Weretiger that is charmed by you and agrees to obey your verbal commands for 24 hours. Afterwards, the Weretiger may become hostile or neutral to you, depending on how you treated it in this state.

name: Five of Coins
effect: Roll 1d100. If the result is less than 60, you lose the corresponding number of gold pieces. If the result is greater than 60, you receive the corresponding number of gold pieces. If you don't have enough gold, it's taken from a random creature within 20 yards; if it succeeds on an Insight check (DC 15), the creature notices this and decides that you are to blame for the missing money. If no such creature is found either, or it doesn't have enough money, you take 3d8 necrotic damage, which ignores resistances and cannot be undone by other means.

name: Seven of Coins
effect: Roll 1d100. On a result of 1 all your money and treasures, as well as the money of all creatures in a circle within 20 ft. radius of you disappear. On a successful Insight check (DC 15), the creature notices this and decides that you are to blame for the missing money. If you score 100, you gain 1 large diamond worth 1000 GP. On a result of 2-99 you receive the corresponding number of gold pieces.

name: Nine of Coins
effect: Roll 1d100. If you score 1, you lose all magical items that belong to you, even those you do not currently wear. If the result is 2-99, you receive the corresponding number of gold pieces multiplied by 10. If you score 100, you receive one Rare or Very Rare Wondrous Item of the Master's choice.

name: Five of Glyphs
effect: You gain the effect of the Cure Wounds spell as if it were cast at 1st level. The attribute bonus for this spell is +3.

name: Seven of Glyphs
effect: You gain the effect of the Remove Curse spell.

name: Nine of Glyphs
effect: Your maximum hit points are permanently increased by 1d8.

name: Joker
effect: Three random cards are dealt out of the deck and their effects take effect immediately, consecutively in the order the cards appear.
